docs.title: Welcome to MkDocs
docs.text: For full documentation visit mkdocs.org.

docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
• mkdocs new [dir-name] - Create a new project.
  • mkdocs serve - Start the live-reloading docs server.
  • mkdocs build - Build the documentation site.
  • mkdocs -h - Print help message and exit.

docs.title: Project layout
docs.text:
mkdocs.yml    # The configuration file.
docs/
    index.md  # The documentation homepage.
    ...       # Other markdown pages, images and other files.

I like to drink after

docs.title: Code examples
docs.text:
add_numbers.py
# Function to add two numbers
def add_two_numbers(num1, num2):
    return num1 + num2

# Example usage
result = add_two_numbers(5, 3)
print('The sum is:', result)

docs.title: Check Hedvig Pages after Reboot of a node
docs.text: watch -n 30 'echo -n "$(zgrep -i "Loaded Container:" /var/log/hedvig/logs/hblock/system* | wc -l) of "; ls -ld /hedvig/d*/data/* | wc -l; head -2 /tmp/hblock*; tail -2 /tmp/hblock*'

docs.title: Check Hedvig Pages after Reboot of the whole cluster
docs.text: while true; do clear; echo "=== Check HedvigPages: $(date) ==="; for i in {11..16}; do echo; echo "hostname$i:"; ssh -q hostname$i 'echo -n "$(zgrep -i "Loaded Container:" /var/log/hedvig/logs/hblock/system* | wc -l | sed "s/^/   /") of "; ls -ld /hedvig/d*/data/* | wc -l; head -2 /tmp/hblock* | sed "s/^/   /"; tail -2 /tmp/hblock* | sed "s/^/   /"'; done; sleep 30; done

docs.title: Sequence Diagrams
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
  autonumber
  Server->>Terminal: Send request
  loop Health
      Terminal->>Terminal: Check for health
  end
  Note right of Terminal: System online
  Terminal-->>Server: Everything is OK
  Terminal->>Database: Request customer data
  Database-->>Terminal: Customer data

docs.title: How to change UTA Port Personality
Change adapter mode
docs.text:
cluster1::> system node hardware unified-connect modify -node cluster1-01 -adapter 1a -mode fc* / cna**
fibre channel converged network adapter

docs.title: How to telnet from a Netapp to test port availability
Unlock diag user and set password
docs.text:
ONTAP::> security login unlock -username diag

ONTAP::> security login password -username diag

docs.title: Go into Privileged Mode
docs.text: ONTAP::> set -privilege advanced

docs.title: Change to Diag User
docs.text:
ONTAP::> set diag

ONTAP::> systemshell local

docs.title: Once here you can telnet like normal
docs.text: ONTAP%>telnet mail.domain.com 25

docs.title: To Break out
docs.text: CTRL C and CTRL D

docs.title: Relock Diag Account
docs.text: NETAPP::> security login unlock -username diag

docs.title: Which Network File System (NFS) TCP and NFS UDP ports are used on the storage system?
docs.text:
• The default ports used by the storage controller are as follows:
111   TCP/UDP portmapper
2049  TCP/UDP nfsd
635   TCP/UDP mountd
4045  TCP/UDP nlockmgr
4046  TCP/UDP status
4049  TCP/UDP rquotad
  • It is possible to modify those default ports, therefore to check the currently configured ports on your storage controller please use the following commands,
cDot::*> nfs show -vserver NFSsvm -fields rquotad-port ,nlm-port ,nsm-port ,mountd-port
vserver mountd-port nlm-port nsm-port rquotad-port
------- ----------- -------- -------- ------------
NFSsvm    635         4045     4046     4049

cDot::*>
  • Alternatively show the ports listening on the node with the following
cdot::> network connection listening show -node <node name>
Vserver Name     Interface Name:Local Port              Protocol/Service
---------------- -------------------------------------  -----------------------
Node: node 1
Cluster          node1_clus1:7700                       TCP/ctlopcp
vs01             VS01_lif01:40001                       TCP/cifs-msrpc
VS01             VS01_lif01:135                         TCP/cifs-msrpc
VS01             VS01_lif01:4049                        UDP/unknown
  • These ports list can also be seen from from most unix clients with the command: rpcinfo -p 
     program vers proto   port  service
    100000    2   udp    111  portmapper
    100000    2   tcp    111  portmapper
    100000    3   udp    111  portmapper
    100000    3   tcp    111  portmapper
    100000    4   udp    111  portmapper
    100000    4   tcp    111  portmapper
    100003    3   udp   2049  nfs
    100003    3   tcp   2049  nfs
    100003    4   tcp   2049  nfs
    100005    1   udp    635  mountd
    100005    2   udp    635  mountd
    100005    3   udp    635  mountd
    100005    1   tcp    635  mountd
    100005    2   tcp    635  mountd
    100005    3   tcp    635  mountd
    100021    4   udp   4045  nlockmgr
    100021    4   tcp   4045  nlockmgr
    100024    1   udp   4046  status
    100024    1   tcp   4046  status

docs.title: How to check vscan server status
You received the following alert
docs.text:
affa220-1-cluster::> event log show -node <node> -seqnum <seqnum>

                  Node: <node>
             Sequence#: 5290401
                  Time: 3/3/2025 12:51:24
              Severity: EMERGENCY
                Source: nblade2
          Message Name: Nblade.vscanNoScannerConn
                 Event: Nblade.vscanNoScannerConn: Vserver "<svm>" has no virus scanner connection.
     Corrective Action: Ensure that the scanner pool is properly configured and the AV servers are active and connected to ONTAP.
           Description: This message occurs when ONTAP(R) has no vscan connection for servicing virus scan-requests. This might cause data unavailability if the scan-mandatory option is enabled.

docs.title: Others
docs.text:
#!/bin/bash

# Function to convert blocks to terabytes
blocks_to_tb() {
  local blocks=$1
  local tb=$(bc -l <<< "scale=4; ($blocks * 512) / (1024 ^ 4)")
  echo "$tb"
}

# Function to convert terabytes to blocks
tb_to_blocks() {
  local tb=$1
  local blocks=$(bc -l <<< "scale=0; ($tb * (1024 ^ 4)) / 512")
  echo "$blocks"
}

# Main function
main() {
  while true; do
    echo "Block and Terabyte Converter"
    echo "---------------------------"
    echo "1. Convert blocks to terabytes"
    echo "2. Convert terabytes to blocks"
    echo "3. Quit"
    read -p "Choose an option: " option

    case $option in
      1)
        read -p "Enter the number of blocks: " blocks
        tb=$(blocks_to_tb $blocks)
        echo "$blocks blocks is equal to $tb TB"
        ;;
      2)
        read -p "Enter the number of terabytes: " tb
        blocks=$(tb_to_blocks $tb)
        echo "$tb TB is equal to $blocks blocks"
        ;;
      3)
        echo "Goodbye!"
        exit 0
        ;;
      *)
        echo "Invalid option. Please choose a valid option."
        ;;
    esac
  done
}

main

docs.title: Check current mode
docs.text:
getenforce
Enforcing

docs.title: List SELinux Contexts for Files and Directories
docs.text:
ls -Z /bin/ls
system_u:object_r:bin_t:s0 /bin/ls

The -Z option shows the SELinux context. In this example: * system_u: User * object_r: Role * bin_t: Type * s0: Sensitivity level (if applicable)

docs.title: Check and search denials
docs.text: ausearch -m avc

docs.title: Check current Linux kernel and Debian version
docs.text:
cat /etc/debian_version
uname -mrs

docs.title: Remove all non Debian packages
docs.text: sudo apt list '?narrow(?installed, ?not(?origin(Debian)))'

docs.title: Unhold all packages if any
docs.text: sudo apt-mark showhold | more

docs.title: Update existing packages and reboot the Debian 11 system
docs.text:
sudo apt update
sudo apt upgrade
sudo apt full-upgrade
sudo apt --purge autoremove
reboot

docs.title: Updates repositories
docs.text: sudo sed -i'.bak' 's/bullseye/bookworm/g' /etc/apt/sources.list

docs.title: Update the packages index
docs.text: sudo apt update

docs.title: Prepare for the operating system minimal system upgrade
docs.text: sudo apt upgrade --without-new-pkgs

docs.title: Update Debian 11 to Debian 12 Bookworm
docs.text:
sudo apt full-upgrade

Source: https://www.cyberciti.biz/faq/update-upgrade-debian-11-to-debian-12-bookworm/

docs.title: Check if VT-x/AMD-V is Enabled on ESXi Host
docs.text:
~ # esxcfg-info | grep "HV Support"
         |----HV Support............................................3
            |----World Command Line.................................grep HV Support

The table below explains the possible values of HV Support.

| 0 | VT-x / AMD-V support is not available for this hardware. | | 1 | VT-x / AMD-V might be available on this CPU but it is not supported for this hardware. | | 2 | VT-x / AMD-V is available but is currently not enabled in the BIOS. | | 3 | VT-x / AMD-V is enabled in the BIOS and can be used. |
